message:
🎨 fix: clarify 'Live' card-badge tooltip (#8363) (#8371)

@xonas1101 asked why some cards have a green "Live" badge and
others don't. The badge is only rendered when a card passes
isLive=true to CardWrapper — used today by VClusterStatus and
DrasiReactiveGraph, both of which subscribe to real-time event
streams. Cards without the badge aren't "not live"; they're
polling at their cache interval.

Expand the tooltip so the distinction is readable without reading
the code:

  before: "Showing live data"
  after:  "Streaming live events — this card subscribes to a
           real-time feed. Cards without the badge refresh on a
           polling interval."

Fixes #8363

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -552,7 +552,7 @@
     "demoBadgeTitle": "This card displays demo data",
     "demoModeTitle": "Demo mode enabled - showing sample data",
     "live": "Live",
-    "liveBadgeTitle": "Showing live data",
+    "liveBadgeTitle": "Streaming live events — this card subscribes to a real-time feed. Cards without the badge refresh on a polling interval.",
     "refreshFailed": "Refresh failed",
     "refreshFailedCount": "{{count}} consecutive refresh failures",
     "refreshFailedCount_one": "{{count}} consecutive refresh failure",
